seed_problem: Themed grid network; remove at most k links to maximize the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores achieved delay.
mutation: composite: generalize the single s-t objective to a MULTI-SINK objective (one pump source, p display-tank sinks) and maximize the SUM of shortest pump-to-tank distances after deleting <=k edges, with the connectivity gate requiring every tank (not just one) to stay reachable; network is a grid-plus-bypass multigraph with heavy-tailed pipe costs.
title: Choked Reef: Aquarium Plumbing Interdiction
strategies: do-nothing baseline: shut no valves (original total travel time)
greedy tree-edge cutting: shut the heaviest edges lying on the shortest-path tree toward the tanks, while keeping every tank connected
best-improvement local search: each round, tentatively delete every remaining edge, recompute the true multi-sink total via one Dijkstra, and commit the edge giving the largest feasible increase
randomized multi-restart: perturb the greedy choices and keep the valve set maximizing total delay
